title: Oscars 2026 Best Supporting Actor Predictions
text: Screen Rant's final predictions for the 98th Academy Awards Best Supporting Actor category following the Golden Globes rank the nominees as follows. Predicted Winner is Sean Penn from One Battle After Another. Penn leads the field due to his established credibility with the Academy as a two-time Oscar winner and brings the most cache with the Oscars among this Best Supporting Actor lineup. His performance earned him victories at the Actor Awards and BAFTA, positioning him as the frontrunner. Runner-up rankings include Delroy Lindo (Sinners) in 2nd place - earned his first nomination without major precursor wins, but the film's acclaim may extend to his candidacy. Stellan Skarsgård (Sentimental Value) ranks 3rd - won a Golden Globe but faces reduced momentum after missing BAFTA. Benicio del Toro (One Battle After Another) ranks 4th - despite multiple nominations across major awards, he underperformed at precursor ceremonies. Jacob Elordi (Frankenstein) ranks 5th - faded after early momentum, lacking wins beyond the Critics' Choice Awards. The race remains competitive, with Penn's track record and co-star advantage within a Best Picture contender making him the safest prediction for the March 16 ceremony.
